ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Continued To Apr-28-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Dec-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DISH HOUSING SF'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO DISMISS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to quash service of summons is granted. When a defendant challenges service, the burden is on the plaintiff to prove proper service. The April and October proofs of service do not show valid service on the face of each POS. Plaintiff offers no evidence. Dish Housing SF further seeks dismissal because the case has been pending against it for more than three years without service. The unopposed motion to dismiss all claims against Dish Housing SF is granted for failure to serve the defendant within two years. (See Code Civ. Proc., sections 583.410 & 583.420, subd. (a)(1).) Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
